title: Democracy
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Democracy
text:
Venezuela After Maduro: Q&A with RAND Experts
Many questions remain one month after the dramatic U.S. operation that captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. How will sudden change reshape Venezuela's future? What are the regional impacts? And how might the U.S.-Venezuela relationship evolve?
Countering Russia-style autocracy in Georgia
The Georgia Dream party is embracing illiberalism, despite the pro-European aspirations of most Georgians. In this perspective, we trace this shift and offer options for increased Western support, to avoid a future of Russia-style autocracy.
Rebalancing the Information Ecosystem and Renewing Shared Societal Commitments for Information Use
This paper emphasizes such priorities as proactive measures and future initiatives at the community level that can help people manage a diverse information ecosystem, shore up civic infrastructure, and enhance cognitive security.
AI Is About to Change Our Lives Forever. Luckily We Have a User Manual for That.
The question isn't whether to slow artificial intelligence, but how to ensure it strengthens rather than undermines the democratic equality the Constitution promises.
The Future of Water and Democracy: Assessing and Improving Water Governance in the United States
In this Perspective, we highlight three vehicles for democracy and three threats to democracy that are common in water governance in the United States.
Taiwan's Risky No-Holds-Barred Politics
Taiwan held a massive recall vote on July 26, threatening to remove 24 opposition Kuomintang politicians from the legislature. All the recalls failed. But the recall saga—and how Taiwan got there—portends long-term, structural threats to the country's democracy.
How the United States Can Help to Save Georgia from the Brink
Georgia faces violent protests against the authoritarian Georgian Dream party, prompting calls for increased U.S. and EU support. Many Georgians demand stronger ties with Europe, seeking to preserve their sovereignty and democratic aspirations amid rising oppression.
On Reducing Public Fears and Threats of Political Violence
The 2024 elections were completed peacefully. But that does not mean risks of political violence and unrest are off the table. To mitigate these risks, it is vital to rebuild trust and ensure transparency in governance.
Against Hyping Civil War and Mass Violence
Taking steps to prevent and prepare for violence always makes sense, but it should not be viewed as a suggestion that the United States is on the brink of civil war.
The 76 Dangerous Days Between the Election and the Inauguration
The most dangerous period America faces in terms of election violence isn't before Election Day, but the 76 days after. Rising threats and domestic violence may intensify post-election, fueled by disinformation, claims of fraud, and potential foreign interference.
Protecting Your Sleep During the Election Season
Election stress can impact sleep as news and social media fuel anxiety. Limiting media before bed, engaging in civic activities, and maintaining a sleep routine can improve well-being and resilience during tense times.
